{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether alternative splicing is associated with heart disease. The provided documents from MEDCORP and UMLS focus on various aspects of heart disease, such as myocardial infarction, coronary artery disease, ischemic heart disease, and related pathologies. However, none of the documents mention or discuss alternative splicing as a biological mechanism or its association with heart disease. Alternative splicing is a molecular biology process where different combinations of exons are spliced together to produce multiple protein variants from a single gene. While this process is known to play a role in many diseases, including cardiovascular diseases, the retrieved documents do not contain any information linking alternative splicing to heart disease. Therefore, based solely on the provided documents, there is no evidence to support an association between alternative splicing and heart disease.", "answer_choice": "B"}
